notes: The Anthropic Institute was launched on March 11, 2026, as a new effort to confront the most significant challenges that powerful AI will pose to societies. The Institute is led by Anthropic co-founder Jack Clark, who assumed a new role as Anthropic's Head of Public Benefit. The Institute has an interdisciplinary staff of machine learning engineers, economists, and social scientists, bringing together and expanding three of Anthropic's research teams: the Frontier Red Team, Societal Impacts, and Economic Research. The Institute launches with about 30 people and Clark expects the think tank's number of staff will double every year.

name: Existential Risk Observatory
notes: The Existential Risk Observatory is a nonprofit foundation founded in May 2021 in Amsterdam, The Netherlands, committed to reducing human extinction risks by informing the public debate. The organization employs 5 people and focuses on areas including unaligned AI, man-made pandemics, climate change, nuclear war, and other existential risks. The Observatory conducts research, media work, lobbying, and organizes events such as conferences on existential risks. Their work includes tracking public awareness of AI existential risk, finding that 24% of the US public is now aware of AI xrisk as of their most recent survey.

name: Goodfire
notes: Goodfire is an AI research company using interpretability to understand, learn from, and design AI systems, with a mission to build the next generation of safe and powerful AI. The company raised $150 million in Series B funding at a $1.25 billion valuation in February 2026, led by B Capital. Goodfire has developed a platform called Ember that decodes neurons inside AI models and lets users precisely shape their behaviors. The company has partnered with organizations like Arc Institute, Mayo Clinic, and Microsoft to deploy their interpretability technology.

name: OpenAI
notes: OpenAI is an American artificial intelligence research organization comprising both a nonprofit foundation and a controlled for-profit public benefit corporation, headquartered in San Francisco. The company was founded in December 2015 as a nonprofit, with a for-profit subsidiary created in 2019, and restructured in 2025 to operate more independently from the nonprofit. OpenAI's mission is to ensure that artificial general intelligence benefits all of humanity. The company has 4,500 employees as of 2026 and reported revenue of US$13.1 billion in 2025.

name: AI Safety Foundation
notes: The AI Safety Foundation is a registered Canadian charity based in Canada that increases awareness and scientific understanding of the catastrophic risks of AI, by advancing education and research, in order to ensure a safer future for humanity. The AISF is proud to present an annual lecture series, the Hinton Lectures™, delivered by world-leading AI safety experts. The organization is supported by generous individuals, corporations and partners. Geoffrey Hinton serves as a Director and is described as Nobel Laureate and "Godfather of AI" who hosts The Hinton Lectures.

name: Cyborgism
notes: Cyborgism is a strategy for safely accelerating alignment research by setting up human-in-the-loop systems which empower human agency rather than outsource it. The approach focuses on training and empowering 'cyborgs', a specific kind of human-in-the-loop system which enhances and extends a human operator's cognitive abilities without relying on outsourcing work to autonomous agents. Connor Leahy coined the term 'cyborgism'. The core of cyborgism is to reduce bandwidth constraints between humans and GPT in order to make deep integration possible.

name: Effective Thesis
notes: Effective Thesis is an organization that aims to empower students and early-career individuals to focus their research and careers towards solving the world's biggest challenges. The organization was founded in July 2017 by David Janku, with initial support from Daniel Hnyk and members of the Czech EA Association. Since then, they have directly supported over 2,000 students across all services and coached more than 500 students one-on-one. The organization is based on the principles of Effective Altruism and is informed by the career advice organisation, 80,000 Hours.

name: How to pursue a career in technical AI alignment
notes: How to pursue a career in technical AI alignment is a comprehensive guide written by Charlie Rogers-Smith. The guide is written for people who are considering direct work on technical AI alignment and is expected to be most useful for people who are not yet working on alignment. The guide was published on June 4th, 2022 and provides information on types of alignment work, how to pursue different paths, and resources for learning AI alignment. It has been referenced and acknowledged by other major AI safety career resources from organizations like 80,000 Hours.

name: Aether
notes: Founded 2024/2025 as independent research group. 3 full-time researchers: Rohan Subramani (starting PhD at University of Toronto), Rauno Arike, and Shubhorup Biswas. $200k funding through December 2025 from anonymous donor. Advised by experts from Astera Institute, Apollo Research, Google DeepMind. Research agenda focuses on enhancing monitorability of LLM agents, with published work on chain-of-thought monitoring and hidden reasoning taxonomy. Currently hiring 1-2 additional researchers.

name: Google DeepMind
notes: Google DeepMind brings together two of the world's leading AI labs — Google Brain and DeepMind — into a single, focused team led by CEO Demis Hassabis. The company's mission is to build AI responsibly to benefit humanity, working to advance artificial general intelligence (AGI). Google DeepMind has achieved notable breakthroughs including AlphaGo defeating world champion Go players, AlphaFold solving protein folding problems, and developing the Transformer architecture that underlies most large language models. The organization employs over 1,000 researchers and engineers from diverse disciplines and maintains partnerships with academic institutions, healthcare organizations, and government agencies.

name: Modeling Cooperation
notes: Modeling Cooperation conducts long-term future research on improving cooperation in competition for the development of transformative artificial intelligence (AI). They build upon AI competition models to analyze ways to shift competitors' incentives toward more safety. The organization received a $77,116 grant from the Foresight Institute for their ongoing collaboration with Technology Strategy Roleplay (TSR). Modeling Cooperation is fiscally sponsored by Convergence Analysis.

name: Softmax
notes: Softmax is an AI alignment research company co-founded by Emmett Shear (cofounder and ex-CEO of Twitch, ex-interim-CEO of OpenAI) that is building a mathematical theory of 'organic alignment' - developing AI systems that can align with each other and with humans organically, through emergent cooperation, rather than via hard-coded, hierarchical control. The company is focused on AI agents discovering their 'values' even as those agents meet and aim to surpass human intelligence. Softmax has Ken Stanley as an advisor and collaborates with research partners.

name: Equilibria Network
notes: Equilibria Network is a Technology, Information and Internet company that describes itself as 'Designing New Forms Of Collective Intelligence'. The organization employs 2 people and has presence in United States and Sweden. They are running a workshop and research program on phylogeny of agents during autumn 2025. The company focuses on exploring the vast design space of possible coordination mechanisms and helping evaluate emergent risks and safety of multi-agent AI systems.

name: Beneficial AI Foundation
notes: Beneficial AI Foundation (BAIF) is a nonprofit organization founded in 2023 with the mission "TO ENSURE THAT AI TECHNOLOGY DOESNT CAUSE HARM". The organization supports AI safety research through university partnerships and its Buterin Fellowship program, with research areas including mechanistic interpretability, audit standards, and red-teaming. BAIF manages the Quantitative AI Safety Initiative (QAISI), a multi-university partnership pursuing quantitative guarantees that AI will not cause harm. The organization is led by Max Tegmark as President and Treasurer and operates the Buterin AI Existential Safety Fellowship program in partnership with Future of Life Institute.

name: Cadenza Labs
notes: Cadenza Labs is an AI Safety research group consisting of four SERI-MATS alumni who work on interpretability research. The organization focuses on doing research which contributes to AI Safety, with their main goal being to research, benchmark and develop robust lie detectors for LLMs. They received funding from LTFF until the end of September 2023 and have been seeking additional funding to continue their work and expand their team. The group has produced research including work on LIARS' BENCH, a testbed for evaluating lie detection techniques in large language models.

name: RAND Corporation
notes: RAND Corporation is a nonprofit, nonpartisan research organization that provides leaders with the information they need to make evidence-based decisions. The organization studies the potential opportunities and risks of artificial intelligence, including ways to strengthen the AI workforce, countering AI-driven social media manipulation by U.S. adversaries, AI governance and how to make future AI systems safer, potential military applications of AI, how AI is being used in the classroom, and identifying occupations at risk of being replaced by AI. RAND operates the RAND Center on AI, Security, and Technology (RAND CAST), which is at the forefront of charting the path for a flourishing future in an era of rapid technological change. The organization was founded in 1948 as an independent nonprofit corporation, separating from the Douglas Aircraft Company.

name: Geodesic Research
notes: Geodesic Research is a UK-based technical AI Safety organization focused on compute-intensive alignment research. The organization was founded by students at the University of Cambridge and employs 6 people. Geodesic Research published a paper titled "Alignment Pretraining: AI Discourse Causes Self-Fulfilling (Mis)alignment" which found that LLMs pretrained on data about misaligned AIs become less aligned, while pretraining with synthetic data about good AIs helps them become more aligned. The organization is a project of Meridian Cambridge.

name: AI Objectives Institute
notes: The AI Objectives Institute (AOI) is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit R&D lab founded by the late internet security pioneer Dr. Peter Eckersley. The organization focuses on harnessing the potential of large-scale systems, including AI, markets, and bureaucracies, to advance humanity's interests. AOI was founded in 2021 and employs 13 people. The institute collaborates with and employs working groups of academics, scientists, economists, entrepreneurs, and government officials.

name: Ergo Impact
notes: Ergo Impact appears to be referenced in multiple contexts across different sources. According to source, Ergo Impact is listed as a funding supporter for RAND's Geopolitics of AGI Initiative, alongside other donors like Good Ventures, Open Philanthropy, and Jaan Tallinn. Source indicates that Effective Giving rebranded to Ergo Impact. Source shows Ergo Impact operates as ergoimpact.org and describes itself as helping UHNW philanthropists find and fund solutions to pressing problems, laying the foundation for what became Ergo Impact.

name: Forecasting Research Institute
notes: The Forecasting Research Institute develops forecasting methods to improve decision-making on high-stakes issues. The organization employs 41 people and operates as a 501(c)(3) research nonprofit. FRI was founded in 2021 with initial support from Coefficient Giving. The institute's flagship project is the Existential Risk Persuasion Tournament (XPT), which brought together 169 participants who made forecasts about existential threats including AI, biosecurity, climate change, and nuclear war.

name: Epoch AI
notes: Epoch AI is a nonprofit research institute founded to investigate the trajectory of artificial intelligence through empirical analysis of historical trends in machine learning. The organization was co-founded in June 2022 by Jaime Sevilla and Tamay Besiroglu, among others, with Sevilla serving as the organization's Executive Director. Epoch AI's mission is to improve society's understanding of the drivers, progress, and impact of artificial intelligence. The organization focuses on building a shared scientific foundation for thinking about AI, neutral to any specific agenda, so decisions about this technology are informed by the best available data.

name: Macroscopic Ventures
notes: Macroscopic Ventures is a Swiss nonprofit founded in 2019 that makes grants and investments to projects and people helping to build a future guided by reason and compassion. The organization employs 6 people and is headquartered in Basel, Switzerland, operating in 5 countries including United States, Japan, United Kingdom, and Canada. Their focus areas include artificial intelligence governance and policy, AI welfare, cooperative AI, societal long-term risks, and animal welfare. The organization was previously known as Center for Emerging Risk Research (CERR) and adopted its current name in February 2025.

name: Effective Institutions Project
notes: The Effective Institutions Project is a philanthropic advisory and research organization that seeks out and incubates high-impact strategies to improve institutional decision-making around the world. AI governance has been a core focus for the organization since its founding in 2021, and the majority of the organization's resources are currently devoted toward making progress on this important and timely issue. The organization has 12 employees according to LinkedIn data. EIP works in partnership with funders and civil society organizations across the globe to coordinate efforts, share knowledge, and drive towards coherent and broadly endorsed policy outcomes that reduce the risks of transformative AI and enable society to flourish.

name: Cooperative AI Foundation
notes: The Cooperative AI Foundation (CAIF) is a charitable entity backed by a $15 million philanthropic commitment from Macroscopic Ventures. CAIF's mission is to support research that will improve the cooperative intelligence of advanced AI for the benefit of all. The foundation is registered with the Charity Commission for England and Wales under charity number 1201294. CAIF employs 16 people with a 77.8% year-over-year growth.

name: Lionheart Ventures
notes: Lionheart Ventures is an early-stage venture capital firm focused on the wise development of transformative technologies. Their main areas of focus are artificial intelligence and frontier mental health. The firm was founded in 2019 and is based in San Francisco. Their AI portfolio includes Softmax, Anthropic, Goodfire, Gray Swan, Piramidal, and Lucid Computing.

name: Meta Charity Funders
notes: Meta Charity Funders (MCF) is a funding circle that aims to fund charitable projects working one level removed from direct impact. MCF is a network of donors sharing knowledge, discussing funding opportunities, and running joint open grant rounds in the EA meta space. The organization received approximately 60 applications in Spring 2025 and ultimately funded 8 projects, with members granting approximately $530,000 in that round. Members are expected to give above $100,000 to this cause area each year, which is the expected minimum annual giving to join.

name: Mythos Ventures
notes: Mythos Ventures is an early-stage venture capital firm based in San Francisco, California, founded in 2023. The firm closed $14 million in capital commitments for its first fund to invest in transformative AI companies. Mythos Ventures announced Fund II as a $30M pre-seed fund backing founders building AGI-proof companies. The firm employs 5 people and is headquartered at 1410 Franklin St, San Francisco.

name: Senate AI Working Group
notes: The Senate AI Working Group is a bipartisan organization formed in the 118th Congress, consisting of Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer (D-NY), Senator Mike Rounds (R-SD), Senator Martin Heinrich (D-NM), and Senator Todd Young (R-IN). The Working Group was formed due to shared recognition of the profound changes artificial intelligence could bring to the world and the need to proactively work to harness opportunities and address risks of this transformational technology. The Working Group's objective has been to complement the traditional congressional committee-driven policy process, considering that AI technology does not neatly fall into the jurisdiction of any single committee. On May 15, 2024, the Working Group released a report titled "Driving U.S. Innovation in Artificial Intelligence: A Roadmap for Artificial Intelligence Policy in the United States Senate".

name: Juniper Ventures
notes: Juniper Ventures is a venture capital firm that focuses on investing in startups that enhance the safety and security of artificial intelligence. The firm spun out of Climate Capital to invest in synthetic biology for the climate, with founding partners Michael Luciani and Jennifer Kan who had been investing in climate for years as part of Climate Capital. Their first fund is $10.6 million and is oversubscribed, with limited partners including family offices, foundations, and Allocator One serving as the anchor. The firm operates by writing checks between $100,000 and $500,000 to scientists who are working to commercialize their research.

name: Oxford Martin AI Governance Initiative
notes: The Oxford Martin AI Governance Initiative aims to understand and anticipate the lasting risks from AI through rigorous research into the technical and computational elements of AI development, combined with deep policy analysis. The Initiative is co-led by Professor Robert Trager, a social scientist specialising in international relations and frontier AI regulation, and Professor Michael Osborne, a specialist in machine learning. The Oxford Martin AI Governance Initiative employs 30 people (+65.0% YoY, +13 people) and operates in 7 countries (including United Kingdom, United States, China, France, and Argentina). The Initiative is shaping global AI governance debates, with agenda-setting research informing policy and regulatory discussions across the UN, EU, UK and US.

name: AI Standards Lab
notes: AI Standards Lab is a remote organization that aims to accelerate AI safety standards writing in organizations like ISO/IEC, CEN-CENELEC, the IEEE, and NIST. The organization employs 10 people and is structured as a virtual lab bringing together volunteers from around the world. AI Standards Lab has actively participated in the EU AI Act's General-Purpose AI Code of Practice drafting process, submitting detailed technical feedback across multiple drafts. The organization has published extensive reports analyzing EU AI Act amendments and providing recommendations on various regulatory proposals.

name: TruthfulAI
notes: TruthfulAI is a non-profit organization that researches situational awareness, deception, and hidden reasoning in language models. The team is led by Owain Evans and is based in Berkeley, California. The organization has published multiple papers including work on emergent misalignment published in Nature and the TruthfulQA benchmark. TruthfulAI works towards safe and aligned AI systems.

name: SaferAI
notes: SaferAI is a French non-profit furthering risk management in policy and industry. SaferAI works across research and policy to make AI safer by advancing responsible AI development through modeling complex risks, improving company practices, and contributing to standards and policies. SaferAI has been selected by the European Commission's AI Office to provide technical assistance on assessing risks posed by GPAI models at EU level, winning Lot 1 of the 'Technical Assistance for AI Safety' tender. SaferAI released its first ratings evaluating the risk-management practices of top AI companies, with xAI receiving the lowest score of 0/5.

name: Alignment Ecosystem Development (AED)
notes: Alignment Ecosystem Development (AED) is an AI safety field-building nonprofit focused on building and maintaining valuable digital infrastructure for the AI safety ecosystem. AED has built, taken ownership of, closely partnered with, and/or maintained approximately 15 different projects, including the AI Safety Map (aisafety.world), AI Safety Info (aisafety.info), and AI Safety Quest (aisafety.quest). The Long-Term Future Fund provided a $99,330 grant to AED through their fiscal sponsor, Ashgro Inc., covering a 1-year stipend for 1.25 full-time equivalents to build and maintain digital infrastructure. AED has received zero funding so far prior to this grant and has until now been entirely volunteer driven.

name: AI Now Institute
notes: The AI Now Institute was founded in 2017 as a research institute studying the social implications of artificial intelligence and policy research that addresses the concentration of power in the tech industry. As of mid-2022, AI Now is operating as an independent organization and does not accept funding from corporate donors, including the tech industry we aim to hold accountable. The institute's co-executive directors are Amba Kak and Sarah Myers West. AI Now's leadership were invited to advise the US Federal Trade Commission on artificial intelligence in 2021.

name: Workshop Labs
notes: Workshop Labs is a research lab focused on user-aligned models with a mission to make people irreplaceable. The company is building technology to train billions of models, each designed for their user, and describes itself as a public benefit corporation making AI that works for you, not AI that automates you for someone else. Luke Drago is identified as the co-founder and CEO of Workshop Labs. Rudolf Laine and Luke Drago are co-founding Workshop Labs according to a LinkedIn post from Rudolf Laine.

name: The Verge
notes: The Verge is about technology and how it makes us feel, founded in 2011. The Verge was founded in 2011 in partnership with Vox Media, and covers the intersection of technology, science, art, and culture. The Verge is an ambitious multimedia effort to examine how technology will change life in the future for a massive mainstream audience. The site is powered by Vox Media's Chorus platform, a modern media stack built for web-native news in the 21st century.

name: European Network for AI Safety (ENAIS)
notes: The European Network for AI Safety (ENAIS) is a dynamic community of researchers and policymakers from across Europe, united in their efforts to advance AI safety. ENAIS brings together experts from over 13 countries and counting, all working towards ensuring that artificial intelligence systems are safe, ethical, and positively impactful for humanity. ENAIS distinguishes itself from related organizations by its focus on existential risks from AI. ENAIS is funded by Lightspeed grants.

name: xAI
notes: xAI is an American AI company founded by Elon Musk on March 9, 2023, with a mission to "understand the true nature of the universe". SpaceX acquired xAI in February 2026 in an all-stock transaction that valued SpaceX at $1 trillion and xAI at $250 billion. The company's flagship product is Grok, an AI chatbot that is accessible on grok.com, iOS, and Android. xAI raised $20 billion in a Series E funding round in January 2026, with strategic investors including Nvidia and Cisco Investments.

name: RAISEimpact
notes: Program of Successif launched in 2024-2025, fully funded by Open Philanthropy grant through May 2025. Led by Adam Tury (former Amazon tech leader, EA coach) and Patrick Gruban (board member of Effective Ventures UK). Currently serves 4 AI safety organizations as clients, eligibility requires organizations working on AI GCRs with 5+ paid staff. Offers 4-step process: discovery call, leadership workshop, recommendations review, and implementation support including conflict management, hiring consulting, and leadership coaching.

name: Safe AI Forum
notes: Safe AI Forum (SAIF) is a US 501(c)3 non-profit that aims to foster international collaboration to reduce AI risks. The organization was co-founded by Fynn Heide and Conor McGurk in late 2023. SAIF's flagship program is the International Dialogues on AI Safety (IDAIS), which brings together senior computer scientists and AI governance experts to build international collaboration on these risks. SAIF started as a fiscally sponsored project at FAR.AI in 2023 and has now successfully transitioned to operating as an independent non-profit as of May 2nd 2025.

name: Trajectory Labs
notes: There are multiple organizations named Trajectory Labs with different focuses. One is a nonprofit based in downtown Toronto focused on AI safety, funded by Open Philanthropy and operational since January 1st, 2025. Another is described as a stealth AI security startup working with leading AI companies on red-teaming. A third appears to be a product studio that builds software, mobile apps, and web applications. There is also a company that builds safety evaluations and RL environments for frontier AI labs.

name: Safe Superintelligence Inc.
notes: Safe Superintelligence Inc. is an Israeli-American artificial intelligence company founded by Ilya Sutskever, the former chief scientist of OpenAI; Daniel Gross, former head of Apple's AI efforts; and Daniel Levy, an investor and AI researcher. The company was founded on June 19, 2024, with the goal to safely develop superintelligent AI. SSI raised $1 billion from venture capital firms including SV Angel, DST Global, Sequoia Capital, and Andreessen Horowitz in September 2024. In March 2025, SSI reached a $30 billion valuation in a funding round led by Greenoaks Capital.

name: Timaeus
notes: Timaeus is an AI safety and alignment research nonprofit founded in October 2023, with the mission of making breakthrough scientific progress on AI safety using singular learning theory. The organization was co-founded by Jesse Hoogland (Executive Director) and Daniel Murfet, who recently left a tenured professorship in algebraic geometry to pursue this work full-time. Timaeus has grown from a team of four with an initial budget of $145k to a current team of eight (soon up to 12) with around $2.5M in funding for 2025. The organization focuses on applying singular learning theory to foundational problems within alignment, particularly through their "developmental interpretability" research agenda.

name: Forethought (AI Safety Research Nonprofit)
notes: Forethought is a nonprofit research organization focused on how to navigate the transition to a world with superintelligent AI systems. The organization was cofounded by Max Dalton, Will MacAskill, Tom Davidson, and Amrit Sidhu-Brar. They are a small research nonprofit that aims to tackle the most important questions they can find, unrestricted by the current Overton window. The organization was built out of the old Forethought Foundation for Global Priorities Research to help get the operations started.

name: Narrow Path (by ControlAI)
notes: Narrow Path (by ControlAI) is a comprehensive plan to address extinction risks from artificial superintelligence, structured in three phases: Phase 0 (Safety), Phase 1 (Stability), and Phase 2 (Flourishing). The plan was published in October 2024 and is being actively pushed to lawmakers in the UK and US through ControlAI's Direct Institutional Plan (DIP). Andrea Miotti is identified as the Executive Director of Control AI and the lead author of A Narrow Path. The plan proposes preventing the development of artificial superintelligence for 20 years while building international oversight systems.

name: FAR.AI
notes: FAR.AI is a research & education non-profit founded in 2022 that focuses on ensuring advanced AI is safe and beneficial for everyone. The organization is led by CEO Adam Gleave and COO Karl Berzins. FAR.AI secured over $30 million in funding commitments throughout 2025 from supporters including Coefficient Giving (previously Open Philanthropy), Schmidt Sciences, Survival and Flourishing Fund, the Center for Security and Emerging Technology (CSET), and the AI Safety Fund (AISF) supported by the Frontier Model Forum (FMF). The organization was selected by the European Commission's AI Office to conduct technical safety research supporting the implementation of the EU's landmark Artificial Intelligence Act.

name: Intelligence Rising
notes: Intelligence Rising is both a documentary film directed by Elena Andreicheva (2026) and a separate training workshop organization that provides strategic simulations of AI futures. The documentary features a geopolitical war game exploring AI-driven crises with participants including thinkers from the EU, China, and the United States. The training workshop organization is headquartered in London, United Kingdom and employs 10 people, offering educational roleplay games for decision-makers to experience tensions in AI development. There is also a separate Australian company called Intelligence Rising founded in 2018 by Neil Quarmby that provides online courses for intelligence professionals.

name: AI Safety Camp
notes: AI Safety Camp (AISC) is an online part time AI safety research program where participants join projects and work 10 hours/week with weekly meetings. The program runs as a 3-month long online program from January to April, where participants form teams to work on pre-selected AI Safety projects. AI Safety Camp has a seven-year track record of enabling participants to try their fit, find careers and start new organizations in AI Safety, hosting up-and-coming researchers outside the Bay Area and London hubs. The organization is headquartered in Diemen, North Holland, Netherlands and employs 58 people.

name: AI Safety, Ethics and Society (Center for AI Safety)
notes: AI Safety, Ethics and Society is a course offered by the Center for AI Safety (CAIS) that offers 'a comprehensive introduction to how current AI systems work, their societal-scale risks, and how to manage them'. The course is described as 'Training 1000+ future AI safety leaders'. Dan Hendrycks authored a work titled 'AI Safety, Ethics, and Society' published by the Center for AI Safety in 2024. The course appears to be part of CAIS's broader mission to 'reduce societal-scale risks associated with AI by conducting safety research, building the field of AI safety researchers, and advocating for safety standards'.

name: Cooperative AI Summer School
notes: The Cooperative AI Summer School is an advanced programme for early-career professionals already working in or studying cooperative AI, taking place over five intensive days with talks from leading researchers and collaborative projects. The 2025 edition was held on the banks of the River Thames in Marlow, England, gathering over 65 students and early-career professionals from across the world. The Cooperative AI Foundation is committed to building a diverse and inclusive research community and is registered with the Charity Commission for England and Wales under charity number 1201294.

name: Alignment of Complex Systems Research Group (ACS)
notes: The Alignment of Complex Systems Research Group (ACS) is an interdisciplinary research group at Charles University studying questions about multi-agent systems composed of humans and advanced AI systems. ACS aims to develop understanding of advanced AI systems and the complex ways they interact with humans and human institutions, seeking to contribute to making such systems safe and aligned towards shared human values like freedom, kindness and justice. Their research draws on insights and methods from machine learning, information theory, network theory, active inference, philosophy of science, ecology and more, with a team that includes philosophers and ML engineers, physicists and cognitive scientists. ACS led the work on the 'Gradual Disempowerment' paper and is listed as a key organization working on gradual disempowerment research.

name: Institute for AI Policy and Strategy (IAPS)
notes: The Institute for AI Policy and Strategy (IAPS) is a nonpartisan think tank that produces policy research to address the implications of AI, from today's most advanced models to potential AGI and superintelligence. IAPS is an initiative of the California-based 501(c)(3) think tank Rethink Priorities (RP) and receives fiscal support from RP as well as operational support from RP's Operations Department. The organization employs 36 people according to LinkedIn data. IAPS's work is primarily funded by a mix of philanthropic grants and individual gifts—including support to date from Open Philanthropy, the Survival and Flourishing Fund, allied government, and individual donors.

name: Foresight Institute
notes: Foresight Institute is a research organization founded in 1986 by Christine Peterson and Eric Drexler. The organization works to advance science and technology for the benefit of life through grants, events, prizes, and fellowships. Foresight Institute is led by CEO Allison Duettmann and COO Karolina Eklöw. The organization focuses on six areas including Secure AI, Neurotechnology, Longevity, Biotechnology, Nanotechnology, Space and Existential Hope.

name: Leaf: Dilemmas and Dangers in AI
notes: Leaf: Dilemmas and Dangers in AI is an interdisciplinary online fellowship helping teenagers explore how to steer cutting-edge AI technology toward benefits for humanity. The program is designed for smart, curious, and ambitiously altruistic students aged 15-19 who haven't yet started university. 97% of participants would recommend the course, with 97% of 300+ Finalists ranking 7/10 or above on their likelihood to recommend this course. The fellowship runs for 5 weeks covering topics from AI foundations to personal impact strategies.

name: Mentorship for Alignment Research Students (MARS)
notes: MARS (Mentorship for Alignment Research Students) is a part-time programme where teams of 2–4 work with experienced mentors to produce published research, providing a kick-off week in Cambridge, compute, and research management. The program is run by Cambridge AI Safety Hub (CAISH) and is a volunteer-based program connecting aspiring researchers with experienced mentors to conduct AI-safety research. Previous participants have published at NeurIPS, ICML, EMNLP, and ControlConf, with mentorship from researchers at Redwood Research, Apollo Research, Google DeepMind, the University of Cambridge & other organisations. MARS research has been accepted at top venues like NeurIPS and ICLR.

name: Supervised Program for Alignment Research (SPAR)
notes: The Supervised Program for Alignment Research (SPAR) is a part-time, virtual mentorship program that connects mentors and mentees for three-month AI safety and governance research projects. SPAR is run by Kairos, a nonprofit whose mission is to help society navigate the transition to transformative AI. The program accepts mentees with relevant technical or policy background at any level, from undergraduate students to mid-career professionals. SPAR research has been accepted at ICML and NeurIPS, covered by TIME, and led to full-time job offers for mentees.

name: From AI to ZI
notes: From AI to ZI is a Substack blog by Robert Huben, a Ph.D. mathematician and independent AI safety researcher. Supported by an Open Philanthropy grant (Oct 2022–Sep 2023) to learn and contribute to AI safety, the blog covers interpretability, alignment theory, and existential risk for general audiences. It has been semi-dormant since the grant ended in late 2023.

name: GiveWiki
notes: GiveWiki is described as "Your Crowdsourced Charity Evaluator" and "The AngelList for philanthropy" that allows users to follow expert grantmakers with their donations. The platform was formerly known as "Impact Markets" and was rebranded to GiveWiki in 2023. GiveWiki operates as a community-driven platform where users register their donations and the platform aggregates them into a list of top charities ranked by their community buy-in. The platform initially focused exclusively on AI safety projects but later reopened to all cause areas in 2024.

name: OpenBook
notes: OpenBook is a grants database tracking effective altruism funding, including substantial AI safety investments. Shows major donors like Open Philanthropy ($1.5B total), Jaan Tallinn (hundreds of millions to AI safety orgs like AI Impacts $546K, Fund for Alignment Research $524K), and recipient organizations across the AI safety ecosystem. No longer actively maintained according to AISafety.com map. Provides transparency into funding flows between major EA funders and AI safety research organizations.

name: Machine Learning for Alignment Bootcamp (MLAB)
notes: Intensive 3-4 week bootcamp program run by Redwood Research. Two iterations completed in 2022 (MLAB1 in January with 28 participants, MLAB2 in August with ~40 participants). Currently inactive with no plans for future iterations, but curriculum materials available. Covers PyTorch, transformers, interpretability, RL with focus on hands-on implementation. Several participants went on to work full-time in AI alignment.

name: London Initiative for Safe AI (LISA)
notes: Founded September 2023 as UK charity. Operates coworking hub hosting organizations like Apollo Research, BlueDot Impact, and training programs like MATS, ARENA. Led by team including James Fox (Research Director, Oxford PhD), Mike Brozowski (Operations Director). Funded by Open Philanthropy, seeking $3.7M annually. Houses 120+ researchers from 50+ organizations. Three-pillar approach: mobilize talent, strengthen organizations, convene ecosystem.

name: Meta AI
notes: Meta AI is a research division of Meta (formerly Facebook) that develops artificial intelligence and augmented reality technologies. The division was founded on December 11, 2015, with founders including Rob Fergus, Yann LeCun, Serkan Piantino, and Mark Zuckerberg. Meta AI has workspaces in Menlo Park, London, New York City, Paris, Seattle, Pittsburgh, Tel Aviv, and Montreal as of 2025. The organization produces products including Llama and operates under the ownership of Meta Platforms.

name: Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI)
notes: The Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI) is a body within the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) at the U.S. Department of Commerce. It was established on June 3, 2025 when Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick renamed and refocused the U.S. AI Safety Institute (AISI), which had been created under the Biden administration in November 2023 by Commerce Secretary Gina Raimondo. The rebrand dropped "safety" from the title and pivoted the mission from catastrophic-risk research toward national security, cybersecurity, chemical/biological weapons misuse evaluation, and global competitiveness with China. CAISI serves as industry's primary U.S. government point of contact for voluntary testing and collaborative research on commercial AI systems, develops security guidelines, conducts capability and vulnerability evaluations, and represents U.S. interests in international AI standards bodies. Inaugural AISI director Elizabeth Kelly — who helped author the 2023 Biden AI executive order — led the institute from February 2024 until her departure on February 5, 2025 following the Trump transition; no public successor director has been confirmed as of early 2026. Under Kelly, AISI reached pre-deployment testing agreements with OpenAI and Anthropic and launched the Testing Risks of AI for National Security (TRAINS) Taskforce in November 2024. Paul Christiano and other AISI-era staff were hired prior to the rebrand.

name: AE Studio
notes: AE Studio is a bootstrapped software development, data science, and design agency founded in 2016, with approximately 120-150 employees working to increase human agency through technology. The company reinvests profits from its consulting business into AI alignment research and development, focusing on what they call 'neglected approaches' to solve the AI alignment problem. AE Studio has evolved from a tech consultancy to pioneers in AI alignment research, also working extensively in brain-computer interfaces (BCI) and collaborating with companies like Blackrock Neurotech. The company is self-funded and independent, building what they describe as a 'Bell Labs-style research engine' focused on actually solving alignment at the root.

name: AISafety.com
notes: AISafety.com is described as a curated resource hub focused on preventing human extinction from AI, maintained by AI safety community-builders. The organization is run by a global team of volunteers and professionals from various disciplines who believe AI poses a grave risk of extinction to humanity. The project lead is Søren Elverlin, and the site was recently redesigned with Bryce Robertson involved in the redesign effort. AISafety.com provides various resources including events, communities, courses, funding opportunities, and job listings for those looking to contribute to AI safety.

name: Center for AI Safety (CAIS)
notes: The Center for AI Safety (CAIS) is an American nonprofit organization based in San Francisco that promotes the safe development and deployment of artificial intelligence. It was founded in 2022 by Dan Hendrycks and Oliver Zhang. CAIS' mission is to reduce societal-scale risks associated with AI by conducting safety research, building the field of AI safety researchers, and advocating for safety standards. In May 2023, CAIS published the statement on AI risk of extinction signed by hundreds of professors of AI, leaders of major AI companies, and other public figures.

name: SAG-AFTRA
notes: SAG-AFTRA represents approximately 160,000 actors, announcers, broadcast journalists, dancers, DJs, news writers, news editors, program hosts, puppeteers, recording artists, singers, stunt performers, voiceover artists and other entertainment and media professionals. SAG-AFTRA was formed in 2012 through the merger of Screen Actors Guild (founded 1933) and American Federation of Television and Radio Artists (founded 1937). The union is a proud affiliate of the AFL-CIO and has national offices in Los Angeles and New York with local offices nationwide. SAG-AFTRA has been actively negotiating AI protections for performers, including updated rules in 2026 that require informed consent for digital replicas and appropriate compensation.

name: Andreessen Horowitz (a16z)
notes: Andreessen Horowitz (a16z) is a private American venture capital firm founded in 2009 by Marc Andreessen and Ben Horowitz. The firm raised over $15 billion across multiple funds in 2025, representing over 18% of all venture capital dollars allocated in the United States that year. a16z has over $90B+ under management across multiple funds as of March 5, 2026. The firm focuses on investing across AI, bio + healthcare, consumer, crypto, enterprise, fintech, games, infrastructure, and companies building toward American dynamism.

name: AI Futures Project
notes: AI Futures Project is a nonprofit research organization founded in 2024 that specializes in forecasting the development and societal impact of advanced artificial intelligence. The organization is best known for its 2025 scenario forecast, AI 2027, which examines the potential near-term emergence of artificial general intelligence (AGI) and its possible global consequences. The organization employs 5 people and is funded by charitable donations and grants. Their current core team includes Daniel Kokotajlo, Eli Lifland, Thomas Larsen, Romeo Dean and Lauren Mangla.

name: Mistral AI
notes: Mistral AI is a French artificial intelligence company headquartered in Paris, founded in April 2023 by three AI researchers: Arthur Mensch, Guillaume Lample, and Timothée Lacroix. The company develops open-source large language models and AI systems, with a mission to democratize artificial intelligence through open-source, efficient, and innovative AI models, products, and solutions. As of 2025, the company has a valuation of more than US$14 billion and employs over 800 team members from 30+ different nationalities. Mistral AI has secured significant funding including a €2 billion investment in September 2025 that valued the company at €12 billion ($14 billion).

name: DeepSeek
notes: DeepSeek was founded in 2023 in Hangzhou, China by Liang Wenfeng, who previously co-founded one of China's top hedge funds, High-Flyer, which focuses on AI-driven quantitative trading. The company released its first AI large language model later in 2023. DeepSeek operates independently but is financially backed by High-Flyer, receiving a significant $50 million investment from the hedge fund with no institutional or angel investors. The company is fully funded by High-Flyer, which manages approximately Rmb60 billion (around $8 billion) as of 2023.

name: Compassion in Machine Learning (CaML)
notes: Compassion in Machine Learning (CaML) is a research organization that conducts research on aligning Artificial Intelligence with the well-being of all sentient beings. The organization focuses on using Synthetic Document Finetuning to shape AI behaviors more carefully, making them more robustly compassionate and morally thoughtful. CaML employs 3 people with presence in Mexico and Germany. The organization is a project of Players Philanthropy Fund, Inc., a Texas nonprofit corporation recognized by IRS as a tax-exempt public charity under Section 501(c)(3).

name: NYU Alignment Research Group (ARG)
notes: The NYU Alignment Research Group (ARG) is a set of researchers doing empirical work with language models that aims to address longer-term concerns about the impacts of deploying highly-capable AI systems. The group overlaps and works closely with a variety of groups at NYU, including Machine Learning for Language (ML2), CILVR, the Center for Data Science, and The Department of Linguistics. The group was set up as a new lab by Sam Bowman, who stated 'I'm setting up a new lab! It's called the NYU Alignment Research Group'. Current leadership includes Sam Bowman as PI (on leave), with He He and Mengye Ren as Collaborating PIs, and Ethan Perez as Outside advisor.

name: Apollo Research
notes: Apollo Research is an evaluation organization focusing on risks from deceptively aligned AI systems, founded in 2023 by Marius Hobbhahn and others. The organization conducts technical research on AI model evaluations and interpretability, with expertise in LLM agent evaluations for strategic deception, evaluation awareness and scheming. Apollo Research partners with frontier labs, multinational companies, governments, and foundations, including collaborations with OpenAI and the UK's AI Safety Institute. The organization demonstrated that frontier AI models including OpenAI o3, Claude 3.5 Sonnet, and others are capable of scheming behaviors in controlled tests.

name: Alignment Research Center (ARC)
notes: The Alignment Research Center (ARC) is a non-profit research organization whose mission is to align future machine learning systems with human interests. ARC was founded in 2021 by Paul Christiano. The organization is based in Berkeley, California and operates as a 501(c)(3) tax exempt charity. ARC's current research focus involves trying to produce formal mechanistic explanations for neural network behaviors in order to produce robustly aligned systems.

name: Center for Human-Compatible AI (CHAI)
notes: The Center for Human-Compatible AI (CHAI) is a research center at the University of California, Berkeley focusing on advanced artificial intelligence safety methods. The center was founded in 2016 by a group of academics led by Berkeley computer science professor and AI expert Stuart J. Russell. CHAI's mission is to develop the conceptual and technical wherewithal to reorient the general thrust of AI research towards provably beneficial systems. The center has 23 employees according to LinkedIn data.

name: Conjecture
notes: Conjecture is an AI safety/alignment research startup founded by Connor Leahy, Sid Black and Gabriel Alfour, which aims to scale alignment research. The company is building a new AI architecture called Cognitive Emulation to ensure the controllable, safe development of advanced AI technology. Conjecture has VC backing from investors including Nat Friedman, Daniel Gross, Patrick and John Collison, Arthur Breitman, Andrej Karpathy, and Sam Bankman-Fried. The company is located in London and was founded as a for-profit company with products on the market.

name: BlueDot Impact
notes: BlueDot Impact is a non-profit organization based in London that serves as the leading talent accelerator for beneficial AI and societal resilience. Since 2022, they have trained over 7,000 people worldwide through their courses on AI safety, governance, and biosecurity. The organization has raised $35M in funding and their alumni work at organizations like Anthropic, Google DeepMind, and the UK's AI Security Institute. They offer free courses including 'Future of AI', 'Frontier AI Governance', and 'AI Alignment'.

name: Secure AI Project
notes: Secure AI Project is a Public Policy Offices company that employs 6 people and is classified as a nonprofit organization. The organization advocates for legally requiring large AI developers to publish safety and security protocols, protect whistleblowers who reveal unsafe practices, and provide clear incentives for developers to mitigate risk in accordance with industry best practices. Secure AI Project co-sponsored California's SB 53 bill, which was signed into law and establishes world-leading AI safety disclosure requirements. The organization has supported legislation in nine states including Utah, Nebraska, Tennessee, Michigan, Colorado, New York, Massachusetts, Illinois, and California, working with both Democratic and Republican sponsors.

name: New Consensus
notes: New Consensus is an agile think tank focused on designing comprehensive, detailed plans for governments to achieve economic renewal and transformation. The organization was founded in 2017 and employs 3 people, with a -60.0% year-over-year change. New Consensus is the author of the Green New Deal and Mission for America. The organization's guiding principle states that "It is possible to create a world where the people of every nation and community control the means to sustainably make a prosperous living".

name: Center for a New American Security (CNAS)
notes: The Center for a New American Security (CNAS) is an independent, bipartisan, nonprofit organization that develops strong, pragmatic, and principled national security and defense policies. CNAS was established in 2007 by co-founders Dr. Kurt M. Campbell and Michèle A. Flournoy. The organization is located in Washington and engages policymakers, experts, and the public with innovative, fact-based research, ideas, and analysis to shape and elevate the national security debate. CNAS has strong ties to the Democratic Party and was formerly led by CEO Victoria Nuland, who served as undersecretary of state for political affairs from 2021 to 2024 in the Biden administration's State Department.
